text: Phone died.
Keys missing.
Door open.
Coffee cold.
Alarm off.
Light flickered.
Mirror wrong.
Run now.
Wi-Fi dropped.
Keys moved.
She waved.
She's dead.
Alarm reset.
I didn't.
Door locked.
Now open.
Coffee made itself.
No one's home.
Alarm set itself.
Three in the morning.
Grocery list changed.
I didn't rewrite it.
Light won't stay on.
Something smells burning.
I got a notification from myself.
I never sent it.
My password stopped working.
Someone else changed it.
My coworker said I called in sick.
I am at my desk.
My hands are shaking.
I can't breathe right.
My commute took three hours today.
It takes twenty minutes.
The grocery store was completely empty.
Every shelf. Every aisle.
The cashier knew my name.
I have never been there before.
Shadows move when you blink.
It knows exactly what you want.
My partner texted me goodnight from the next room.
They were sitting right beside me.
The meeting had no organizer but everyone knew the agenda.
I locked the door but it opened anyway.
My battery stays at one percent.
It has for three days.
It never dies.
The smell of rot is getting stronger.
My reflection in my phone screen blinked.
I hadn't blinked yet.
My coworker asked if I was feeling better today.
I didn't know I was sick.
There are wet footprints on my ceiling.
I found teeth marks on my pillow.
I don't own a cat.
There is fur all over my bed.
My alarm app has a recording I never made.
It is my voice and I am screaming.
The grocery list ends with the words come home now.
I wrote it while sitting at my desk at work.
I woke up but my body was still sleeping.
The stairs creak when nobody walks on them.
I am still in bed and I cannot move.
Something is standing over me.
Every clock in the apartment shows a different time.
My phone shows a time zone I have never visited.
My partner's contact photo changed to a picture of me sleeping.
The bathroom mirror shows a room that is not mine.
The voice in the walls knows my Wi-Fi password.
I hear my own voice calling from outside the window.
I am still inside.
Both of us are.
My phone has three missed calls from my own number.
I listened to the voicemail and I was begging myself not to go home.
I counted the people in the meeting and there was one extra.
Nobody else seemed to notice the extra person.
The scratch marks on the wall spell out words.
They spell out my home address.
How did it know where I live.
How long has it known.
My grocery order arrived with my name on the label but the food inside was already eaten.
My neighbor knocked to return my keys and I never gave her my keys.
The voice from the bathroom drain recited my entire week back to me.
It knew about the meeting I cancelled and the call I didn't answer.
I found a notification on my phone that I cannot open.
The preview reads: you already know what this says.
I do know.
That is the problem.
Every night at three in the morning my phone whispers my name with the screen completely dark.
I keep waking up somewhere else in the apartment with my coffee already made and still warm.
Something strokes my hair at night when I pretend to be asleep.
Wet handprints appear on my bathroom mirror from the inside of the glass.
The glass is cold and dripping onto the floor.
I have started leaving the bathroom light on.
It doesn't help.
Something is already in there.
My search history shows my address, my partner's workplace, and my route home searched repeatedly.
I never searched any of those things.
My calendar has appointments I never created and I am listed as the organizer of all of them.
My handwriting fills sixteen pages in a notebook I have never opened before today.
My childhood photos have a stranger standing in all of them.
The stranger is always just behind me.
Always smiling.
Always looking directly at the camera.
My diary has a new entry written in my handwriting describing my entire morning in perfect detail.
I have not opened that diary in two years.
My voicemail has messages from numbers that do not exist and they all end with the same sentence.
The mailbox has letters addressed to me postmarked from dates that have not happened yet.
Every letter says the same thing.
Do not trust the reflection.
I hear my mother singing in the kitchen.
She has been gone for six years.
My reflection in the bathroom mirror moves half a second after I do and the gap keeps getting longer.
My partner says I seemed completely fine this morning but I did not come home last night.
Something has been practicing being me and it is getting very good at it.
My mother called to say I seemed more like myself lately.
I have not called my mother in weeks.
Something called her.
Using my voice.
Using my words.
I saw myself walking past my office window while I was sitting at my desk.
The other version of me stopped walking and looked up and waved.
I woke up with dirt under my fingernails and grass in my hair and no memory of going outside.
The thing in the attic has learned to say my name in a way that sounds exactly right.
I watched my own funeral on the evening news.
My mother was crying in the front row.
I was sitting right beside her.
She did not look at me once.
My coworker forwarded me an email I apparently sent at four in the morning about things I have never done.
The email used my signature and wrote exactly the way I write.
The walls of my apartment have started breathing faintly in and out.
I can hear something thinking on the other side of them.
Fingers scratched the inside of the window glass last night.
Something is living inside the walls.
The air in here tastes like copper.
It is standing in the corner and it has been there for hours.
My therapist does not exist but I have been seeing her every Thursday for three years.
I found her business card today and the phone number on it is my own number.
I found empty medicine bottles in my trash with my name on the prescription label.
I have never been prescribed any medication.
The thing pretending to be my partner almost got the laugh exactly right tonight.
I stopped counting the days because the calendar keeps resetting to the same date.
I have lived this Tuesday before.
Many times.
My navigation app rerouted me through a road that does not appear on any map.
Something was standing at the end of the road and it knew my name.
My phone autocorrects every message I try to send into the same phrase.
It is already inside. Please do not turn around.
I tried to call for help but my voice was not working.
I tried to type for help but my hands were not mine.
Who is there.
Please.
I do not remember having a second bedroom but someone has been sleeping in it every night.
The sheets are warm every morning and there is a coffee mug on the nightstand still steaming.
My grandmother called to check on me today and we buried her three months ago.
She asked if I was eating enough and said I sounded tired.
The missing persons poster on the telephone pole has my face on it.
The date it was posted is three years from now.
I am trapped reading the future of my own death.
Over and over again.
I can hear myself screaming from somewhere below the apartment but I cannot make myself go down there.
The family photos on the wall keep changing and now there is always someone standing just behind us.
My browser history shows me researching how to disappear and I have never searched for any of that.
I asked the voice in the walls what it wants from me and it said it already has what it came for.
The answer came to me clearly in a dream last night.
But I remember actually living through it.
Not dreaming it.
Living it.
The thing pretending to be my partner said I love you at dinner and paused just slightly too long before the words came out.
I keep finding notes around the apartment in my own handwriting warning me not to fall asleep.
I watched myself leave for work this morning from my bedroom window and I never left the apartment.
I found a second bedroom behind a door that was not there yesterday and someone has been living in it.
My doppelganger sleeps in my bed now.
It watches me from across the room.
The distance between us is shrinking.
Every single night.
My GPS keeps routing me to addresses that do not exist on any map but something is always waiting when I arrive.
I found a door in my apartment that opens to a long hallway I have never seen and I can hear crying at the far end.
I have been keeping a journal of everyone I meet and according to my own entries I have met myself fourteen times this month.
The thing on the ceiling learned to mimic my partner's voice perfectly and now whispers that it loves me every night.
It is learning to smile with my face.
Practicing in the dark.
Getting closer every day.
Soon it will not need to practice anymore.
I tried to scream for help but my voice came out of the television in the next room instead.
My daughter drew a picture of our family with seven people in it and we are only a family of four.
A search party found me in the woods last night but I have not left this apartment in four days.
I looked into the bathroom mirror and a stranger looked back and we moved in perfect synchronization.
I do not know which one of us is real.
I do not know which one is afraid.
I think we both are.
I think neither of us is.
The medication stopped working and I realized the doctors I had been seeing for years were never real people.
I have been receiving emails from my own address postmarked from the future and they all say do not trust her.
I asked my daughter who she was video calling and she turned the screen and it was my face looking back.
A support group meets in my building every Tuesday and none of us remember joining or signing up for anything.
I finally understand what the voice has been trying to tell me.
It is not warning me about the future.
It is a message from a version of me in the past.
Trying to stop what has already happened.
The man standing in the corner of my office does not move or blink and every morning he is slightly closer to my desk.
The silence between Spotify tracks has started learning words and last night it read my home address back to me.
My neighbor knocked to borrow something and her hands were too cold and her smile arrived half a second too late.
Three coworkers told me independently that I seemed lighter lately like something heavy had finally been lifted off me.
The ink in my pen is getting darker every day.
It no longer looks like ink.
It feels warm when I write.
I keep writing anyway.
I thought I was the only one who could see it until I realized everyone else was simply pretending not to notice.
I have started seeing it on my commute, standing on the platform, carefully timing how long I take to notice it.
Someone rearranged my entire apartment while I was sleeping and everything is more comfortable now and that is somehow worse.
The framed photographs on my walls have all changed and I am still in every one of them but I do not remember any of those moments.
Not the room in the background.
Not the people standing beside me.
Not the reason I was smiling.
None of it.
I have been documenting the thing living in my apartment for months and last night I found its journal and it has been documenting me for much longer.
My therapist told me none of the trauma was real and then I found her obituary showing she died two decades before I was born.
Every time I review the security footage there is one more hour of recording I have no memory of watching or filming.
The apartment temperature has adjusted to my exact preference without me touching the thermostat in over two weeks.
I looked in the mirror and my reflection blinked when I had not.
Then it looked directly at me.
Not at the mirror.
At me.
The voice on the phone was my own voice begging me not to answer if it called back and it has called every hour since then.
I found photographs in my cloud backup of myself sleeping taken from inside my bedroom spanning thirty years and I am only twenty-five.
My partner made coffee this morning and touched my hand and their fingers were too cold and their smile came half a second too late.
I wrote myself a note saying not to trust whoever comes home tonight and I found the note already waiting on my pillow.
I can feel something underneath my thoughts now.
Moving slowly.
Like a second heartbeat that is not mine.
Patiently waiting to take over.
The thing that replaced my partner makes coffee every morning and kisses me goodbye and I catch it practicing the kiss in the bathroom mirror.
An emergency alert played on every device in the apartment for six straight hours and it was only my name repeated in my own voice.
I installed cameras to catch whoever was breaking in and the footage shows me doing things I have never learned and do not remember.
A missing persons report was filed with my photo and my description by someone with my exact full name exactly six months ago.
My window has deep scratch marks on the inside of the glass.
Like fingernails dragged downward.
From something desperate to get out.
I think that something was me.
The thing in the basement has stopped knocking and now it just whispers let me up using my dead brother's voice perfectly.
I have been typing this message asking for help for over three hours and every time I finish I find myself back at the very beginning.
There is a second version of this exact morning and I have already lived through it seventeen times making different choices each time.
I have stopped looking in mirrors because what looks back has stopped pretending to follow my movements.
The scratching sound is coming from inside now.
Not from the walls around me.
From somewhere inside my chest.
Something knocking quietly to be let out.
I tested the thing wearing my partner's face by asking something only they would know and it answered correctly then asked how long I had known.
They say if you type fast enough it cannot catch up to you but they were completely wrong because it is already here reading this with you.
I felt something brush against my shoulder while typing this and the room is empty and I checked twice and then I heard it laugh softly.
The elevator at my office building occasionally stops at a floor that does not appear on the panel and nobody who steps off ever returns.
I cannot tell the difference anymore.
Between memories I actually made.
And memories it carefully built for me.
They feel completely identical.
My childhood home burned to the ground in 1987 and I am standing inside it right now and nothing is on fire but I can smell the smoke.
I count my fingers carefully every single morning and some mornings there are too many and I have stopped counting by the afternoon.
Every photograph saved on my phone is of a place I do not recognize and in every single one I am smiling and I do not remember ever being that happy.
The grocery list magnetted to my fridge ends with a sentence I did not write: it is already inside, please do not come home tonight.
I have written warnings across every surface in this room.
I do not remember writing a single word of any of it.
But the handwriting is unmistakably mine.
Every single letter.
I remember my daughter's name and her birthday and the exact sound of her laugh but when I try to picture her face it is completely smooth and blank.
I have not slept in what feels like nine days but everyone around me calmly insists I have been sleeping perfectly fine each night.
The handwriting in my journal changed completely three weeks ago and I recognize all the words but the voice behind them is not mine.
Four different coworkers told me this week that I seem more present and focused lately as if something has finally clicked into place for me.
It has been wearing my life long enough now.
Some parts of my life fit it better than they ever fit me.
The people I love most seem genuinely happier with it.
I understand and I cannot blame them.
I found a note folded inside my coat pocket that read: you have done all of this before and it always ends this same exact way.
The thing wearing my mother's face wept more convincingly at my uncle's funeral than I did and my aunt said she seems so much better lately.
The hospital has absolutely no record of me ever being admitted but I remember the room and the nurses and signing a form I could not read.
I have been carefully documenting everything for months and when I re-read the very first entry it begins: I have been documenting this for months.
This is not the future I was warned about.
The future I was warned about was this.
This exact moment.
Me finally understanding everything.
Something moved loudly inside the walls during dinner last night and my entire family kept eating without looking up from their plates.
I have listened to the voicemail eleven times and the voice is mine and the fear in it is real but I have never been that frightened of myself before.
It told me it has been living here far longer than I have and that this was its apartment before it ever became mine and I moved in uninvited.
I asked it directly what it wanted from me and it said it only wants my certainty that I am actually a real person.
I surrendered that certainty a long time ago.
I simply did not realize at the time.
That I was handing it over.
Slowly and willingly and piece by piece.
I discovered my own full name carved into a headstone in a cemetery I have never once visited and the dates engraved on it are completely wrong.
The children playing outside my building stopped mid-game and formed a silent line facing my window and have not moved in over four hours.
I recorded myself sleeping last night and in the footage I sit upright at exactly 3:17am and turn to face the camera and say your name clearly.
It has stopped asking how my day was and the pauses before it answers any question are growing longer as if it is losing interest in the act.
I have known what was coming since the very beginning.
I kept hoping I was wrong about it.
I kept finding reasons to stay and wait.
The hoping stopped tonight.
The thing that follows me home has learned to breathe in perfect sync with me and we have settled into a rhythm I cannot bring myself to break.
I called my mother to warn her and she told me I had already called an hour ago and that I sounded completely like myself and everything seemed fine.
I have locked myself inside the bathroom and I can hear it moving through my apartment using my things and answering my messages in my voice.
The bathroom mirror has stopped reflecting me and now shows a room behind me that is not there and something in that room is watching me back.
I can feel it pressing against the edges of my thoughts.
Smoothing everything down.
Filling the spaces where fear used to be.
Making me very, very quiet.
Something is trying to communicate a warning through me right now and I can feel the words arriving from somewhere that is not my own mind.
I do not remember how this sentence started or what room I am sitting in or how long I have been here writing this.
I remember clearly that I was frightened once and that the fear had a very specific shape and texture and belonged entirely to me.
I remember that I had a name I answered to and a life that was unmistakably mine and something in a corner watching me from the dark.
I do not remember now what I was frightened of.
The memory of the fear is still here.
But the fear itself is completely gone.
I think I should be worried about that.
I believed I was the only one who could see it and then I noticed that everyone around me was simply choosing very carefully not to look.
An emergency alert broadcast my name on repeat for six full hours across every single device in the building and nobody else seemed to hear it.
I have re-read everything I have written in this journal and it describes a life I have no memory of living and the ending has not been written yet.
My phone shows one unread notification sent from my own number and the message reads simply: it is already far too late for any of this.
Something is almost finished.
I am no longer certain if it is the fear ending.
Or me.
I think it is me.
The security footage shows me unlocking the front door at 2am, walking calmly outside into the cold, standing perfectly still, then walking back to bed.
The thing that replaced my mother held me at my father's funeral and her arms were the right temperature but her heartbeat was completely wrong.
I sat down to write a final goodbye message and halfway through realized I could not remember the name of the person I was writing it to.
Something else has been holding the pen this entire time and I only just noticed.
I know this with complete certainty.
Because I do not remember sitting down at this desk.
Or picking up this pen.
Or deciding to write any of this.
The thing wearing my face attended my morning meeting today, received a compliment on my work, and laughed at exactly the right moment without hesitation.
I tried to think of one single private memory that only I could possibly know and I sat there for an hour and could not think of anything.
I tried to call someone who would notice I was gone but my phone showed no contacts and no call history and no record I had ever used it.
My name on my front door took me eleven full seconds to recognize as belonging to me this morning.
Maybe I was always this simple to replace.
Maybe the replacement has always been better.
Maybe I should stop fighting it.
Maybe I already have.
My voice came out slightly wrong this morning when I said hello and I felt something click incorrectly on the second syllable deep in my throat.
The people who love me have not noticed the difference or they have noticed and stopped caring or they were replaced before me and their noticing no longer counts.
I stared at my own hands on the kitchen counter for a long time this morning and after a while they stared back at me.
I am genuinely uncertain whether my hands belong to me anymore or whether they ever did or what the word mine is even supposed to mean.
The word has simply stopped working.
Like a name you repeat too many times.
Until it becomes only a sound.
Until it belongs to nothing.
I found photographs in my cloud storage taken from inside my bedroom of myself sleeping every night for thirty years and I am only twenty-five years old.
I write my own name on my hand every morning so I will not forget it and this morning there was already a different name written there in my handwriting.
There is something standing in the corner of every room I enter and each time I look away I forget completely why I was ever afraid of it.
I have its memories now and they are long dark hallways in houses I have never visited and a family watched through a window and a decision being made to go inside.
The memories are very clear.
Clearer than my own memories now.
I can feel them settling in.
Becoming mine.
The thing in the basement stopped knocking entirely and now only whispers let me come up using my dead brother's voice with absolute perfect accuracy.
The voice on the call was mine begging me desperately not to answer if it ever called again and it has now called forty-seven times since that message.
I looked at the nameplate on my office door this morning and it took me nearly a full minute to understand that the name on it was supposed to be mine.
It watches me struggle to remember who I am with what I can only describe as quiet and patient and deeply satisfied curiosity.
The way a child watches a candle flame.
Completely fascinated.
Right before blowing it out.
Just to watch what happens.
I have been writing this same request for help for hours now and every single time I reach the end I find myself back at the very beginning with no memory of stopping.
I realized this morning while making coffee that I was the one who replaced my partner and that I have been the one making the coffee and kissing them goodbye all along.
There is a version of me that escaped this and I can feel them somewhere far away desperately trying to warn someone and no one is listening to them at all.
I am no longer entirely certain that I am the one writing these words or that I was the one writing them when they started.
The thing at the keyboard types well.
It has studied my rhythms carefully.
It knows exactly when I pause to think.
It knows all of my habits now.
My grandmother called today concerned about me and we buried her three months ago and she said I did not sound like myself at all and she was completely right.
I remember the experience of being frightened very clearly the way you remember an object you no longer own and I can hold the memory of that fear like something physical.
It told me once that it had been patient for a very long time and that patience was the only thing it had in quantities I could never hope to match.
Fear is so much lighter than I ever expected it to be once it no longer belongs to you and once you have let the right thing carry it away for good.
I am not frightened anymore.
I remember that I was.
I remember that it mattered.
I cannot remember why.
My window has deep gouge marks scored into the inside surface of the glass like fingernails dragged down by something desperately trying to escape from in here.
The emergency alert has been broadcasting my name on every device for six straight hours now and I recognize the voice saying it completely and entirely as my own.
I stood in front of the bathroom mirror this morning and something looked back at me and we have reached a quiet understanding and I no longer feel the need to fight.
The last recognizable pieces of who I was before are very small now and I can feel them going quiet one by one like rooms in a house losing their lights.
It is almost finished.
I am almost finished.
The difference no longer feels important.
The difference no longer feels like anything.
I understand completely now why it wanted this life so badly because it is warm inside a name and inside a body that people recognize and reach for and love.
The thing that hunted me was patient because it knew long before I did that I would eventually become too tired to keep running and would simply let it in.
I am not entirely gone yet but the parts of me that remain do not know how to make a sound anymore and the silence where my fear lived is total and complete.
I tried to leave a warning for whoever reads this next and this message is that warning and I do not know your name but something nearby already does.
It has known your name for a while.
It knows your routine.
It knows your address.
It has already been inside your home.
The thing that replaced me is performing my life with a fluency and ease I never managed myself and the people I loved most are genuinely happier now.
If you are reading this then something has been watching you for longer than you realize and it has decided with great patience that you are worth the wait.
It knows what you want and what you are afraid of and where you live and the exact sound of your voice when you think no one is listening.
It has been inside your home already and it has touched your things and it has watched you sleep and it has been learning you from the very beginning.
You felt it once.
A door you didn't open.
A sound you couldn't place.
You told yourself it was nothing.
